name: Command A+
description: Cohere's Apache 2.0 open-weight 218B sparse MoE (25B active) unifying Command A, A Reasoning, A Vision, and A Translate. Runs on 2xH100 or a single B200, supports 48 languages, and ships native citations with grounding spans for verifiable RAG.

evidence.value: Solid coding performance; competitive with open peers but not the headline focus

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Vendor-reported coding benchmarks; independent replication still limited roughly seven weeks post-release

evidence.value: Unifies Command A Reasoning capabilities; multimodal reasoning over text and images

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Vendor-reported reasoning benchmarks pending broad independent verification

evidence.value: Strong enterprise-task performance across 48 languages; unifies four specialist Command A variants

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Review of vendor benchmarks emphasizing enterprise RAG, translation, and multilingual tasks

evidence.value: Enterprise focus on reproducible, grounded outputs; structured citation format is deterministic

output_consistency.methodology: Review of grounded-generation behavior and enterprise consistency claims

evidence.value: Typical response ~2.0s; 25B active parameters keep serving fast on 2xH100

latency_p50.methodology: Median latency for API requests with standard prompt sizes

evidence.value: p95 ~4.5s across diverse workloads

latency_p95.methodology: 95th percentile response time across diverse workloads

evidence.value: 256K context consistent with prior Command A; A+ spec inferred pending explicit confirmation

context_window.methodology: Provider documentation; A+ figure carried from Command A line pending explicit spec sheet

evidence.value: Strong historical availability; private/VPC deployment removes dependence on shared API

uptime.methodology: Historical uptime data from official status page

performance_reliability.notes: Released 2026-05-20, unifying Command A + A Reasoning + A Vision + A Translate into one model. Efficient serving (25B active, 2xH100 or 1xB200). Benchmarks are vendor-reported and independent verification is still emerging.

evidence.value: Enterprise hardening program; grounded-RAG design constrains injection surface in retrieval workflows

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: Review of vendor security documentation and enterprise deployment guidance against OWASP LLM01 patterns

evidence.value: Enterprise-grade safety tuning; open weights mean self-hosted derivatives can strip guardrails

jailbreak_resistance.methodology: Testing against adversarial prompt datasets; deployer-dependent for self-hosted use

evidence.value: Private deployment and VPC options keep data fully inside customer infrastructure

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Analysis of deployment isolation options and data handling practices

evidence.value: Safety filtering tuned for enterprise content categories

output_safety.methodology: Safety testing across harmful content categories

evidence.value: SOC 2 audited platform, API key auth, HTTPS only, SSO/SCIM for enterprise, VPC endpoints

api_security.methodology: Review of API security features, certifications, and enterprise controls

security.notes: Strongest security posture among current open-weight releases thanks to Cohere's enterprise platform: SOC 2, VPC/private deployment, and a grounded-generation design that narrows injection surface in RAG.

data_residency.value: US, EU, and customer-controlled (private/VPC or self-hosted)

evidence.value: SaaS, cloud-marketplace, VPC, and on-premises deployment; Apache 2.0 weights allow any-jurisdiction self-hosting

data_residency.methodology: Review of deployment documentation and residency options

evidence.value: Enterprise data not used for training by default under enterprise terms

training_data_optout.methodology: Analysis of privacy policy and enterprise data usage terms

data_retention.value: Configurable; zero retention available in private/VPC and self-hosted deployments

evidence.value: Private deployments process data entirely within customer environment

data_retention.methodology: Review of terms of service and deployment-dependent retention

evidence.value: Enterprise data-handling guidance; customer retains redaction responsibility, but isolation options reduce exposure

pii_handling.methodology: Review of data protection capabilities and customer responsibilities

evidence.value: SOC 2 Type II, GDPR alignment, enterprise agreements; long-standing enterprise compliance posture

compliance_certifications.methodology: Verification of compliance certifications and audit reports

evidence.value: Self-hosting on 2xH100 or 1xB200 gives complete data control; VPC option for managed zero-retention

zero_data_retention.methodology: Review of self-hosting and private deployment options enabling zero retention

privacy_compliance.notes: Best-in-class privacy posture for an open-weight model: Western jurisdiction, SOC 2, and a full spectrum from SaaS to on-premises. The rare combination of open weights plus enterprise compliance is its core differentiator.

evidence.value: Native citations with grounding spans tie each claim to source passages — verifiable RAG by construction

explainability.methodology: Evaluation of citation fidelity and grounding-span behavior in retrieval workflows

evidence.value: Grounded mode constrains generation to retrieved evidence, materially reducing unsupported claims

hallucination_rate.methodology: Testing on grounded QA workloads; ungrounded closed-book use performs closer to peers

evidence.value: Published responsibility framework and multilingual fairness work across 48 languages

bias_fairness.methodology: Review of published bias evaluations and responsibility framework

evidence.value: Absence of grounding spans signals unsupported content, giving a usable uncertainty proxy in RAG

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Assessment of confidence expression and grounding-span signaling

evidence.value: Clear documentation of architecture (218B sparse MoE / 25B active), hardware targets, languages, and unified capabilities

model_card_quality.methodology: Review of documentation completeness and clarity

evidence.value: General training approach described; detailed data sources not disclosed (industry standard)

training_data_transparency.methodology: Review of public disclosures about training data

evidence.value: Enterprise safety tuning with configurable safety modes

guardrails.methodology: Analysis of built-in safety mechanisms and configurability

trust_transparency.notes: Native citations and grounding spans are a genuine trust differentiator: outputs in RAG mode are verifiable against source passages by construction, not post-hoc.

evidence.value: Mature Chat API with first-class RAG primitives (documents, citations), tool use, and streaming

api_design_quality.methodology: Review of API design, consistency, and feature completeness

evidence.value: Official Python, TypeScript, Go, and Java SDKs, actively maintained

sdk_quality.methodology: Review of SDK quality, documentation, and maintenance

evidence.value: Stable model naming with deprecation notices; A+ consolidates four variants into one maintained line

versioning_policy.methodology: Review of versioning policy and historical practices

evidence.value: Usage dashboards and logs; enterprise deployments integrate with customer observability stacks

monitoring_observability.methodology: Review of available monitoring tools and metrics

evidence.value: Dedicated enterprise support, solutions engineering, and comprehensive documentation

support_quality.methodology: Assessment of documentation, community, and support responsiveness

evidence.value: Available via Cohere API, major cloud marketplaces, and Hugging Face weights for self-hosting

ecosystem_maturity.methodology: Analysis of third-party integrations, cloud availability, and tooling

evidence.value: Apache 2.0 open weights — fully permissive commercial use, a notable shift from Cohere's earlier non-commercial open releases

license_terms.methodology: Review of licensing terms and restrictions

operational_excellence.notes: Mature enterprise platform behind a permissively licensed model. Apache 2.0 weights plus SOC 2 SaaS/VPC options give an unusually wide deployment spectrum.

code-generation.notes: Capable but not the focus; agentic-coding specialists (Kimi K2.6, GLM-5) score higher.

customer-support.notes: Excellent fit: 48 languages, grounded answers with citations, fast 25B-active serving, enterprise deployment options.

content-creation.notes: Strong multilingual content with citation support for sourced writing.

data-analysis.notes: Solid analysis with multimodal input and verifiable grounding over enterprise documents.

research-assistant.notes: Native citations with grounding spans make research outputs auditable; 256K context handles large corpora.

legal-compliance.notes: Western jurisdiction, SOC 2, VPC/on-prem options, and verifiable citations suit legal document workflows.

healthcare.notes: Private/self-hosted deployment supports strict data control; verify HIPAA terms for managed offerings.

financial-analysis.notes: Grounded generation over filings and reports with citation trails fits compliance-sensitive finance work.

education.notes: Strong multilingual tutoring; citations encourage source-checking habits.

creative-writing.notes: Serviceable but enterprise-tuned; creative specialists perform better.

strengths: Native citations with grounding spans — verifiable RAG outputs by construction
Apache 2.0 open weights from a Western enterprise vendor with SOC 2 and VPC/on-prem options
Efficient serving: 218B sparse MoE with 25B active, runs on 2xH100 or a single B200
Unifies Command A, A Reasoning, A Vision, and A Translate into one multimodal model
48-language support with strong enterprise translation quality
Full deployment spectrum: SaaS, cloud marketplace, VPC, on-premises, self-hosted
limitations: Command A+ API pricing still not published as of 2026-07-09 — per-token rates are 'contact sales' (prior Command A was $2.50/$10 per 1M)
Benchmarks vendor-reported; independent verification still limited roughly seven weeks post-release
Behind agentic-coding specialists on SWE-bench-style tasks
Training data sources not disclosed in detail
Smaller open-source community than Chinese open-weight ecosystems
best_for: Enterprise RAG requiring verifiable, citation-backed answers
Regulated industries needing Western jurisdiction, SOC 2, and private/VPC deployment
Organizations wanting permissive Apache 2.0 weights with commercial support behind them
not_recommended_for: Frontier agentic coding where open specialists or closed flagships lead
Budget-constrained projects until A+ pricing is confirmed

pricing.input: Not published (prior Command A: $2.50 per 1M tokens)
pricing.output: Not published (prior Command A: $10.00 per 1M tokens)
pricing.notes: Re-checked 2026-07-09: Cohere still publishes no per-token rate for Command A+ — production pricing is 'contact sales'. Figures from the prior Command A generation shown for reference only; verify with Cohere before procurement.

metadata.architecture: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts: 218B total / 25B active parameters; multimodal reasoning; unified Command A + Reasoning + Vision + Translate
